# Ralph v2 Progress Log
Started: Mon 13 Apr 2026 05:04:13 AM EDT
PRD: Transform the SIGNAL terminal into a competitive financial intelligence platform: sync AI backfill to production, fix ticker recommendation pipeline, add dynamic column customization, integrate Polygon.io real-time market data, and build comprehensive company profiles with SEC EDGAR institutional/insider data.
Mode: Adversarial (loop)
---

## Codebase Patterns

- **mypy config**: `pyproject.toml` has `[tool.mypy] ignore_missing_imports = true` — needed because many deps lack stubs (boto3, firebase_admin, etc.)
- **database.py kwargs**: Use `dict[str, Any]` for engine kwargs to avoid mypy narrowing errors when mixing bool/int/type values
- **Script conventions**: Scripts in `scripts/` use `sys.path.insert(0, ...)` + `dotenv` load + `init_db(Config.DATABASE_URL)` + `get_session()` pattern
- **News.link is unique**: The `link` column is the unique identifier for articles across local and production DBs

---

## 2026-04-13 09:05 EDT - US-001 - IMPLEMENTOR — Implemented export_ai_backfill.py

- Created `scripts/export_ai_backfill.py` — exports AI fields as SQL UPDATE statements keyed on `link`
- Supports `--dry-run` (count + preview) and `--output <path>` (full SQL dump)
- SQL wraps in BEGIN/COMMIT, handles NULLs, booleans, float formatting, single-quote escaping
- Fixed `app/database.py` kwargs typing (dict[str, Any]) for mypy compatibility
- Added `[tool.mypy] ignore_missing_imports = true` to `pyproject.toml`
- Verified: 31,067 records exported, mypy clean, all PRD test assertions pass
- **Files changed:** `scripts/export_ai_backfill.py`, `app/database.py`, `pyproject.toml`
- **Environment notes for Tester:**
  - Local SQLite DB has 31,067 AI-analyzed articles — `--dry-run` will show this count
  - `--output` requires a writable path (e.g., `/tmp/ai_backfill.sql`)
  - mypy requires `pip install mypy` (not in requirements.txt by default)
  - 8 pre-existing test failures in `test_tiers.py`/`test_rate_limit.py` (stale tier config values) — not related to this story
---

## 2026-04-13 09:12 EDT - US-001 - TESTER — TEST_PASSED

- **All 5 test assertions passed**, all 5 acceptance criteria met
- Executed `--dry-run`: exits 0, prints "Found 31067 AI-analyzed articles to export" with 10-line preview
- Executed `--output`: exits 0, created 40MB SQL file (37744 lines, 31067 UPDATE statements)
- Verified SQL structure: BEGIN/COMMIT wrapping, all 9 AI fields in SET clauses, WHERE keyed on `link`
- Verified single-quote escaping (`''`), boolean formatting (TRUE/FALSE), float decimals
- mypy: "Success: no issues found in 1 source file"
- pytest: 86 passed, 8 failed (all 8 pre-existing in test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py — stale tier config values)
- Source DB has 0 NULL AI fields across 31067 records — NULL code path not exercised but SQL format is valid
- **Artifacts:** `ralph/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/` (dryrun_stdout.log, export_stdout.log, mypy_stdout.log, pytest_stdout.log, output/ai_backfill.sql)
---

## 2026-04-13 09:17 EDT - US-002 - IMPLEMENTOR — Implemented import_ai_backfill.py

- Created `scripts/import_ai_backfill.py` — imports AI backfill SQL dump into target DATABASE_URL
- Parses UPDATE statements via regex, converts SQL literals back to Python values
- Uses parameterized SQLAlchemy queries for execution (safe from SQL injection)
- Runs in a single transaction with full rollback on error
- Queries target DB to identify articles already having `ai_analyzed=True` — skips them
- `--dry-run` validates SQL and reports matched/skipped/unmatched/error counts without executing
- Handles multi-line reasoning fields and escaped single quotes in SQL literals
- Self-validated: 31,067 statements parsed (0 parse errors), all matched in local DB, all correctly skipped (already analyzed), mypy clean
- **Files changed:** `scripts/import_ai_backfill.py`
- **Environment notes for Tester:**
  - Tester must first generate a SQL dump: `python scripts/export_ai_backfill.py --output /tmp/ai_backfill.sql`
  - Then run: `python scripts/import_ai_backfill.py --dry-run --input /tmp/ai_backfill.sql`
  - When testing against local DB, expect: all 31,067 matched, all 31,067 skipped (already analyzed), 0 would update — this is correct because the export came from the same DB
  - Output includes keywords: `matched`, `skipped`, `errors` (test assertion #2)
  - mypy requires `pip install mypy` (not in requirements.txt by default)
  - 8 pre-existing test failures in `test_tiers.py`/`test_rate_limit.py` — not related to this story
---

## 2026-04-13 09:20 EDT - US-002 - TESTER — TEST_PASSED

- **All 3 test assertions passed**, all 6 acceptance criteria met
- Generated SQL dump prerequisite via `export_ai_backfill.py --output` (31067 records)
- Executed `--dry-run`: exits 0, parsed 31067 UPDATE statements (0 parse errors), printed summary with matched/skipped/unmatched/errors counts
- Executed real import (non-dry-run): exits 0, committed transaction, updated=0, skipped=31067 (all already had ai_analyzed=True) — confirms skip logic works correctly
- Tested malformed SQL input: parser correctly ignores invalid lines, reports unmatched articles for nonexistent links
- mypy: "Success: no issues found in 1 source file"
- pytest: 86 passed, 8 failed (all 8 pre-existing in test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py — stale tier config values)
- **Artifacts:** `ralph/test_results/US-002/attempt-1/` (dryrun_stdout.log, real_import_stdout.log, malformed_dryrun.log, mypy_stdout.log, pytest_stdout.log, ai_backfill.sql, malformed_input.sql)
---

## 2026-04-13 09:25 EDT - US-003 - IMPLEMENTOR — Diagnosed and fixed production AI worker pipeline

- **Audit findings:**
  - `_run_bedrock_analysis()` already correctly called in `refresh_feeds_parallel()` after store + dedup (line 128-129)
  - `BEDROCK_ENABLED` was read in `bedrock_config.py` but not referenced in `worker.py` or `config.py`
  - ECS task definition was missing `MINIMAX_MODEL_ID` in `shared_env`
  - Logging in `_run_bedrock_analysis` lacked start log, elapsed time, and per-article failure counts
- **Changes made:**
  - `app/config.py`: Added `BEDROCK_ENABLED` to `Config` class
  - `app/worker.py`: Logs `BEDROCK_ENABLED` status at worker startup
  - `infra/stack.py`: Added `MINIMAX_MODEL_ID: "MiniMax-M2.7"` to `shared_env`
  - `app/services/feed_refresh.py`: Enhanced `_run_bedrock_analysis()` with structured logging — start log with batch_size, elapsed_seconds on all exit paths, success_count/failure_count tracking, skip log when disabled
- **Self-validation:** import ok, all 5 test assertions pass, mypy clean (2 files), pytest 86 passed (8 pre-existing failures in test_tiers.py/test_rate_limit.py)
- **Files changed:** `app/services/feed_refresh.py`, `app/worker.py`, `app/config.py`, `infra/stack.py`
- **Environment notes for Tester:**
  - Test assertions 1-3 are grep/import checks — run them directly
  - Test assertion 4: `python -m pytest tests/ -v -k 'feed or worker'` — 12 tests, all should pass
  - Test assertion 5: `mypy app/services/feed_refresh.py app/worker.py` — should report "Success: no issues found in 2 source files"
  - AC5 (prod verification) requires deploy: `cdk deploy` + `force-new-deployment` on worker service
  - After deploy, trigger `POST /api/refresh` on production and query for `ai_analyzed=True` on newly fetched articles
  - 8 pre-existing test failures in `test_tiers.py`/`test_rate_limit.py` are NOT related to this story
  - mypy requires `pip install mypy` if not already installed
---

1	"""GET /api/market/<symbol> — real-time market data, company details, and SEC filings."""
2	
3	import logging
4	from datetime import datetime, timezone
5	
6	from flask import Blueprint, jsonify
7	
8	from app.auth.middleware import require_auth
9	from app.middleware.rate_limit import limiter
10	from app.services.edgar_client import EdgarClient
11	from app.services.market_data import PolygonClient
12	
13	logger = logging.getLogger("signal.market")
14	
15	market_bp = Blueprint("market", __name__)
16	
17	# Singleton clients
18	_polygon = PolygonClient()
19	_edgar = EdgarClient()
20	
21	
22	@market_bp.route("/api/market/<symbol>")
23	@require_auth
24	@limiter.limit("60 per minute")
25	def market_snapshot(symbol: str):
26	    """Return real-time price data for a ticker symbol."""
27	    if not _polygon.enabled:
28	        return jsonify({
29	            "error": "Market data service unavailable",
30	            "message": "Polygon.io integration is not configured",
31	        }), 503, {"Retry-After": "60"}
32	
33	    data = _polygon.get_ticker_snapshot(symbol)
34	    if data is None:
35	        return jsonify({
36	            "error": "Ticker not found",
37	            "message": f"No market data available for symbol '{symbol.upper()}'",
38	        }), 404
39	
40	    data["updated_at"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
41	    return jsonify(data)
42	
43	
44	@market_bp.route("/api/market/<symbol>/details")

39	def _normalize_company_name(name: str) -> str:
40	    """Normalize a company name for matching by removing common suffixes and punctuation."""
41	    n = name.upper().replace(".", "").replace(",", "").strip()
42	    for suffix in (" INC", " CORP", " CORPORATION", " CO", " LTD", " LLC", " LP", " PLC", " GROUP"):
43	        if n.endswith(suffix):

93	    def _resolve_cik(self, symbol: str) -> Optional[str]:

118	    def get_institutional_holders(self, symbol: str, limit: int = 20) -> Optional[list[dict[str, Any]]]:

212	    def _parse_13f_for_company(

216	        """Fetch a 13F information table and extract shares/value for the target company."""
217	        if not cik or not adsh or not filename:

220	        try:
221	            cik_int = str(int(cik))

255	
256	                found = True
257	
258	                # Value in dollars (as reported in 13F XML)
259	                val_el = entry.find(f"{{{_13F_NS}}}value")
260	                if val_el is None:

281	
282	        except (ElementTree.ParseError, requests.exceptions.RequestException,
283	                ValueError, KeyError) as e:

399	    def _parse_13dg_ownership(
400	        self, filer_cik: str, adsh: str, filename: str,
401	    ) -> tuple[Optional[float], Optional[int]]:
402	        """Fetch a 13D/13G filing document and extract percent_owned and shares_held."""

404	        shares_held: Optional[int] = None
405	
406	        try:
407	            cik_int = str(int(filer_cik))
408	            adsh_nodash = adsh.replace("-", "")
409	            url = f"{EDGAR_SEC_URL}/Archives/edgar/data/{cik_int}/{adsh_nodash}/{filename}"
410	            resp = self._get(url, timeout=15)
411	
412	            if resp.status_code != 200:
413	                return None, None
414	
415	            # Strip HTML tags for text-based parsing
416	            text = re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", "\n", resp.text)
417	            text = re.sub(r"&\w+;", " ", text)
418	            lines = [line.strip() for line in text.split("\n") if line.strip()]
419	
420	            for i, line in enumerate(lines):
421	                # Item 9: AGGREGATE AMOUNT BENEFICIALLY OWNED
422	                if shares_held is None and re.search(
423	                    r"AGGREGATE\s+AMOUNT\s+BENEFICIALLY\s+OWNED", line, re.I,
424	                ):
425	                    for j in range(i + 1, min(i + 5, len(lines))):
426	                        m = re.search(r"^([\d,]+)$", lines[j])
427	                        if m:
428	                            try:
429	                                shares_held = int(m.group(1).replace(",", ""))
430	                            except ValueError:
431	                                pass
432	                            break
433	
434	                # Item 11: PERCENT OF CLASS
435	                if percent_owned is None and re.search(
436	                    r"PERCENT\s+OF\s+CLASS", line, re.I,
437	                ):
438	                    for j in range(i + 1, min(i + 5, len(lines))):
439	                        m = re.search(r"([\d.]+)\s*%", lines[j])
440	                        if m:
441	                            try:
442	                                percent_owned = float(m.group(1))
443	                            except ValueError:
444	                                pass
445	                            break
446	
447	            if shares_held is not None or percent_owned is not None:
448	                return percent_owned, shares_held
449	
450	        except (requests.exceptions.RequestException, ValueError) as e:
451	            logger.warning("edgar 13D/G parse: %s/%s: %s", filer_cik, adsh, e)

453	        return percent_owned, shares_held
